displayTitle: Reading the Echo: Flaw or Geometry?

beats.narration: An echo is a witness, not a verdict. Cross-examine every weld indication to separate true flaws from innocent geometry.

payload.title: READ THE ECHO
payload.sub: An echo is a witness — cross-examine it.

beats.narration: Planar flaws are mirrors — tall and sharp at the right angle. Porosity scatters: low peaks from any direction. Slag sits between — medium, broad, ragged.
Hold the indication and work the probe — traverse, lateral, orbit, swivel. A mirror collapses as the angle changes; a scatterer barely cares.
Geometry impersonates defects. Root bead plots past centerline, both sides. A root crack or I P plots at centerline. Mismatch is one-sided. Plot every echo.

chips.t: Plot every echo on the joint

beats.narration: Plot it, compare sides, then call it. Geometry lives on the surfaces — anything plotting inside the volume is a flaw until proven otherwise.

payload.sub: Inside the volume? Flaw until proven otherwise.
